sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Let me understand Level 2:
- Top bar has 4 colored 2x2 squares (sky blue, magenta, purple, yellow) on the left and a 4x4 yellow square on the right
- Main area has a blue circle and 8 sky blue dots scattered around
- The sky blue dots might form a path or pattern
Looking at the sky blue dot positions:
(37, 18), (37, 41), (40, 37), (41, 16), (54, 49), (55, 14), (56, 47), (57, 16)
These don't form a simple diagonal like level 1. They seem scattered. Maybe I need to click on them in some order?
The colored squares in the top bar (sky blue, magenta, purple, yellow) might be "collectors" - each sky blue dot needs to be collected by the sky blue square? Or maybe I need to click on the colored squares to "activate" them, and they collect dots of matching color?
But all the dots are sky blue (S), and there's only one sky blue square. So maybe the mechanic is different.
Let me try clicking on one of the sky blue dots to see what happens. I'll start with the one closest to the blue circle.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking on the sky blue dot closest to the blue circle
# Blue circle center is around (27, 33)
# Closest dot: (37, 18) or (37, 41) or (40, 37)
# Let me try (37, 18) first
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 37, 'col': 18}])
print(f"Result: {result}")
print(f"Board changed: {result.get('board_changed')}")
print(f"Gameplay changed: {result.get('gameplay_changed')}")
print(f"No impact: {result.get('no_impact')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
